title: [InstCombine] Fold X - Y + Y * C -> X + Y * (C - 1)

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary examples only show i32 and i8 integer types; i64 width is not explicitly mentioned though the patch is type-generic

alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i64 @sub_plus_mul_i64(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
  %1 = mul i64 %y, 6
  %c = add i64 %x, %1
  ret i64 %c
}
alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i64 @sub_plus_mul_i64(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %a = sub i64 %x, %y
  %b = mul i64 %y, 7
  %c = add i64 %a, %b
  ret i64 %c
}
=>
define i64 @sub_plus_mul_i64(i64 %x, i64 %y) {
#0:
  %#1 = mul i64 %y, 6
  %c = add i64 %x, %#1
  ret i64 %c
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

Summary:
  1 correct transformations
  0 incorrect transformations
  0 failed-to-prove transformations
  0 Alive2 errors

tests.outside_scope_reason: C=1 is an edge case where C-1=0, resulting in multiplication by zero; summary examples only show constants >= 7 or <= -10

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @sub_plus_mul_c1_edge(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %a = sub i32 %x, %y
  %b = mul i32 %y, 1
  %c = add i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %c
}
=>
define i32 @sub_plus_mul_c1_edge(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  ret i32 %x
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.outside_scope_reason: C=0 is an edge case where C-1=-1, producing negative multiplier; summary examples don't demonstrate zero constant

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @sub_plus_mul_c0_edge(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %a = sub i32 %x, %y
  %b = mul i32 %y, 0
  %c = add i32 %a, %b
  ret i32 %c
}
=>
define i32 @sub_plus_mul_c0_edge(i32 %x, i32 %y) {
#0:
  %a = sub i32 %x, %y
  ret i32 %a
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
